trigger_keywords: afirmaciones sobre el dióxido de azufre
inhibidor tanto de levaduras no deseadas como de bacterias
SO2 inhibits unwanted yeast and bacteria

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A winemaker adds an appropriate dose of sulphur dioxide to must or wine.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The antimicrobial fraction of SO₂ interferes with microbial metabolism; its effectiveness depends especially on pH, dose, binding, and the susceptibility of each organism.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Unwanted yeasts and bacteria are inhibited, helping the producer control microbial activity without implying that every organism is eliminated.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Correctly dosed SO₂ can inhibit unwanted yeasts and bacteria, with effectiveness governed by pH, available molecular SO₂, and microbial susceptibility.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
